classification_rationale: The brief explicitly names "afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts" and "TSSD spec" as NORMATIVE artifacts. This repo hosts the canonical VaultedSignalRecord type (src/tssd/types.ts:331), the TSSD_VAULT_SPEC (docs/TSSD_VAULT_SPEC.md) and TSSD_REPLAY_CLI_SPEC.v0.1 (docs/TSSD_REPLAY_CLI_SPEC.v0.1.md) which define the Evidence-plane vault schema, lifecycle semantics (RAW->ENRICHED->ANALYZED->SCORED->MINTED->REPLAYED, types.ts:10-16), public-vs-proprietary surface separation (types.ts:206-230), and replay determinism invariants. These are interoperability rules, not just one stack. However the repo MIXES normative type/spec definitions with a REFERENCE_IMPL (Mongo-only client) and STALE docs (AGENTS.md, .afi-codex.json), so it is normative-primary but impure.
primary_purpose: afi-infra defines AFI's canonical T.S.S.D. Vault types and lifecycle spec (the off-chain per-signal Evidence-plane record) plus signal-stage Zod schemas, agent-role definitions, and a read-only deterministic replay runner; package.json:3 "Infrastructure services, TSSD vault clients, and shared schemas for AFI Protocol".

reference_only_assumptions.quote: The TSSD Vault lives off-chain (e.g. in MongoDB time-series collections), while on-chain receipts ... provide a lightweight, immutable breadcrumb.

### Future: Mongo-Backed Implementation
A production-ready `MongoTSSDVaultClient` will: Leverage MongoDB time-series collections...
if (isProd) { throw new Error("[TSSD] AFI_TSSD_MONGODB_URI is required in production. Falling back to in-memory is not allowed."); }
It should only talk to TSSD (Mongo) for reads, local scoring/analysis code, and optionally dedicated replay/audit collections.

contradictions.title: TSSD vault production path is Mongo-only; multi-engine vault is absent
contradictions.detail: The North Star vault.schema.json expects per-vault Mongo/PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB/InfluxDB pluggability. afi-infra's only persistent client is MongoTSSDVaultClient, the factory hard-fails in prod without a Mongo URI, the spec names Mongo as THE production backend, and zero references to postgres/timescaledb/influxdb exist in the repo. The Evidence-plane reference impl collapses to Mongo-only, the exact 'Mongo tunnel vision' tension.
contradictions.evidence: src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:199 "AFI_TSSD_MONGODB_URI is required in production. Falling back to in-memory is not allowed."; docs/TSSD_VAULT_SPEC.md:133-137 "production-ready MongoTSSDVaultClient ... Leverage MongoDB time-series collections"; grep for postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb returns 0 hits

contradictions.title: Canonical vault type omits pinned-version / determinism replay fields required by north-star rule (2)
contradictions.detail: Decentralization rule (2) requires pinned transforms/plugin/analyst/validator versions recorded FOR REPLAY in the canonical record. VaultedSignalRecord stores only optional analystScore.strategyVersion; there is no pinned DAG topology id, plugin-manifest version, scorer/codex version, or content-hash linkage field at the record level. The replay spec records scorer version at RUN time, not in the canonical document, weakening challenge-ready reproducibility.
contradictions.evidence: src/tssd/types.ts:331-367 (VaultedSignalRecord has identity/stages/publicSurface/proprietaryDetail/training but no pinned-topology/version/contentHash fields); types.ts:108 only "strategyVersion?: string"; replay version capture is run-scoped: docs/TSSD_REPLAY_CLI_SPEC.v0.1.md:18

contradictions.title: Spec claims on-chain receipt carries baseScore+confidence, richer than stated on-chain reality
contradictions.detail: Brief's CURRENT ON-CHAIN REALITY says on-chain stores only lightweight breadcrumbs (signalId, epoch, amounts, beneficiary) and NOT UWR/score breakdowns. TSSD_VAULT_SPEC asserts receipt contains baseScore and confidence, conflating aspirational richer anchors with shipped contracts. Could mislead a validator about what is actually anchored on BASE.
contradictions.evidence: docs/TSSD_VAULT_SPEC.md:103 "Receipt (on-chain): Minimal public fields (signalId, epochId, baseScore, confidence, txHash) for provenance and emissions"

contradictions.title: .afi-codex.json labels the vault CLIENT entrypoint as the canonical vault, conflating type-spec with one impl
contradictions.detail: The codex marks src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts (the concrete client + InMemory/Mongo impls) as role canonical_signal_vault. The canonical artifact per the protocol is the TYPE/SPEC (types.ts + TSSD_VAULT_SPEC), not the storage client. This frames a reference implementation as protocol law.

contradictions.title: DAG determinism test points to afi-reactor doctrine as 'real' orchestration
contradictions.detail: The determinism test (the only DAG artifact here) defers to afi-reactor's AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md as where 'Real DAG orchestration lives', reinforcing the reactor-as-THE-orchestrator framing the audit flags as reference-vs-normative tension. The local mock proves determinism abstractly but does not pin topology/versions.
contradictions.evidence: tests/dag.deterministic.test.ts:15 "Real DAG orchestration lives in afi-reactor (see AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md)."

contradictions.title: Stale AGENTS.md / codex references to deleted dirs and a non-existent consolidation plan
contradictions.detail: AGENTS.md describes a template/stub-oriented repo and references templates/, stubs/, cli_templates/, agent-stubs/, schemas/index.ts and docs/TSSD_VAULT_CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.v0.1.md - all confirmed MISSING after the 'remove legacy stubs' cleanup (commit c880919). README still says Phase 1.5 scaffolding while code ships real Mongo clients. Doc drift can misdirect agents/auditors.
contradictions.evidence: AGENTS.md:3 "This repo is template/stub oriented, not production runtime."; AGENTS.md:58 references TSSD_VAULT_CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.v0.1.md (file absent); .afi-codex.json:50 entryPoint "schemas/index.ts" (absent)

upstream: afi-core (package.json:26 "afi-core": "file:../afi-core"; AnalystScoreTemplate is canonical source per types.ts:98; validator_metadata Zod schema lives in afi-core per agent-roles/validator_metadata_v1.md:30)
afi-config (AGENTS.md:61 "Depends on: afi-config (schemas)"; Charter authority AGENTS.md:5)
downstream: afi-reactor (.afi-codex.json:35 dependsOn; README.md:67 DAG orchestrator consumer)
afi-token (.afi-codex.json:36 dependsOn; receipt/mint integration per TSSD_VAULT_SPEC.md:98)

notable_findings.title: VaultedSignalRecord is the canonical Evidence-plane record and is well-formed for public/proprietary split
notable_findings.evidence: src/tssd/types.ts:331 "VaultedSignalRecord: The CANONICAL record of a signal's full lifecycle."; types.ts:206-230 PublicSurfaceView/ProprietaryDetailView cleanly separate public surface from analyst edge
notable_findings.recommendation: Promote types.ts + TSSD_VAULT_SPEC into afi-docs/specs as the normative vault.schema source of truth; ensure vault.schema.json (afi-config) is generated from or aligned to this type.

notable_findings.title: Persistence layer is MongoDB-only across spec, client, factory, replay (Mongo tunnel vision)
notable_findings.evidence: 68 mongo grep hits; src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts is the sole persistent client; TSSDVaultClient.ts:218 returns MongoTSSDVaultClient; replay spec line 24 "only talk to TSSD (Mongo)"; grep postgresql|timescaledb|influxdb = 0 hits
notable_findings.recommendation: Document that ITSSDVaultClient is the normative contract and Mongo is ONE reference adapter; add a non-Mongo adapter stub or explicit note that Postgres/Timescale/Influx adapters are protocol-valid to dispel Mongo-as-mandatory.

notable_findings.title: Replay runner enforces determinism + read-only-over-canonical invariants but is a v0.1 single-signal stub
notable_findings.evidence: src/tssd/TSSDReplayRunner.ts:5 "read-only, deterministic ... No external network calls, no mutation of canonical TSSD history."; docs/TSSD_REPLAY_CLI_SPEC.v0.1.md:11-25 Determinism/Read-only/Version-awareness invariants; TSSDReplayRunner.ts:50 bulk filters TODO
notable_findings.recommendation: Good replay-determinism foundation; extend record schema with pinned scorer/DAG version + content hash so replays are verifiable against the canonical doc, not just run metadata.

notable_findings.title: Determinism enforced only via local mock; no pinned topology/version pinning in canonical record
notable_findings.evidence: tests/dag.deterministic.test.ts:8-13 "Identical input -> identical output ... Pure functions only"; test uses fixed timestamps; line 15 defers real DAG to afi-reactor
notable_findings.recommendation: Add a record-level pinned-transforms/version field so determinism is provable across stacks (north-star rule 3+5), not just in an in-repo pure-function mock.

notable_findings.title: No USS/CPJ ingest dialect references anywhere in repo
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirm ingest-boundary dialect validation lives in afi-gateway/afi-config; if TSSD RAW snapshots are expected to derive from USS/CPJ, document the mapping (RawSignalSnapshot vs USS v1.1).

notable_findings.title: No mint/receipt contract symbols present; only off-chain MintSnapshot mirror
notable_findings.evidence: grep AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted|MintCoordinated = 0 hits; src/tssd/types.ts:187 MintSnapshot only mirrors on-chain receipt metadata (txHash, tokenAddress, chainId 8453)
notable_findings.recommendation: None - contracts correctly live in afi-token; ensure MintSnapshot field set stays aligned with actual AFISignalReceipt event fields to avoid the doc-vs-contract drift seen at SPEC line 103.

notable_findings.title: Tenant isolation enforced by overwriting analystId; cross-tenant reads return null
notable_findings.evidence: src/tssd/TenantScopedTSSDVaultClient.ts:30-37 upsert forces identity.analystId = tenantId; lines 41-45 getBySignalId returns null if analystId !== tenantId
notable_findings.recommendation: None; note tenant scoping silently rewrites caller-provided analystId on upsert (line 34) which could mask provenance if a caller passes a different analystId - consider asserting rather than overwriting.

notable_findings.title: Mongo time-series collection cannot enforce unique signalId; falls back to non-unique index
notable_findings.evidence: src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:245-254 catch -> "Unique index on identity.signalId not supported on time-series collection; creating non-unique index instead." and upsert does delete-then-insert (lines 156-160)
notable_findings.recommendation: Document that signalId uniqueness (a canonical invariant - 'globally unique identifier', types.ts:23) is NOT DB-enforced in the Mongo adapter; concurrent upserts could duplicate records. This is a reference-impl limitation, not a protocol rule, but should be flagged for replay integrity.

notable_findings.title: Registries/reputation invariants correctly stated: PoI/PoInsight must not override UWR or vault finality
notable_findings.evidence: agent-roles/validator_metadata_v1.md:9 "reputation and PoI/PoInsight never override UWR or vault finality"; line 30 references REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION invariants; types.ts:165 "PoI / PoInsight are NOT stored here"
notable_findings.recommendation: None; this aligns with the north-star decentralization rule that reputation influences selection/allocation only.

notable_findings.title: Repo self-description is stale: AGENTS.md/README call it template/stub Phase-1.5 while code ships real vault clients
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:3 "template/stub oriented, not production runtime"; references missing dirs templates/, stubs/, cli_templates/, agent-stubs/ and missing TSSD_VAULT_CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.v0.1.md; README.md:37 "Phase 1.5"
notable_findings.recommendation: tag stale - update AGENTS.md/README/.afi-codex.json to reflect post-cleanup state (commit c880919) and remove dead path references.
